mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year out-of-sample walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized broad-based demand growth, reinforcing corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at 5% level) or yields a negative Sharpe ratio in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over the next month during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during early-cycle expansions are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal's information coefficient turns negative when filtered by NBER expansion dates.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market strength and rising aggregate income, supporting consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, which consistently generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS increases show no positive predictive power for 20-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over a 10-year out-of-sample period, or the relationship reverses during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4%, or the inflation threshold fails to predict short-term drawdowns in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms robust aggregate demand momentum, reducing recession probabilities and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined positive growth of GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or insignificant predictive coefficient for 20-day returns, or the signal's out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50% in a 5-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising housing starts indicates a structural supply-demand mismatch and sectoral rotation away from cyclical manufacturing, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-HOUST divergence signal produces positive 60-day returns in over 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect loses statistical significance when tested against NBER recession dating.
